mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 55% during rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0%, labor market slack signals weakening aggregate demand and rising recession probability, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.0%, or the t-statistic for return difference is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real short-term asset flows combined with falling federal funds rates indicate liquidity injection into financial markets, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant with p-value above 0.10 in out-of-sample regressions, or signal loses predictive power when FEDFUNDS is flat.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during post-shock recovery phases reflects rapid capacity utilization and earnings revisions, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses or becomes noise during mature expansion phases, with out-of-sample Sharpe ratio falling below 0.5 over 5-day horizons.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect expanding economic activity and corporate revenue growth, generating positive forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.01 across all rolling 2-year windows, or payroll growth shows zero correlation with 20-day equity returns in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts fall below 1500 thousand units, construction sector contraction and falling consumer confidence trigger negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 55% of observations below the 1500 threshold, or the threshold effect vanishes after controlling for interest rates.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price indices alongside slowing real GDP growth indicate stagflationary pressures that compress equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint condition fails to predict returns when CPIAUCSL growth is below 3%, or interaction term loses significance in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Declining unemployment paired with rising industrial production in early-cycle recovery phases signals improving labor absorption and output growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Signal becomes unprofitable during late-cycle phases, with negative Sharpe ratio over 5-day horizons when UNRATE is already below 4.0%.
